'''Joe Stimson''' (born 2 December 1995) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{Rlp|pr|sr}} or {{Rlp|lf}} forward for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Melbourne Storm]], [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Early life==
Stimson was born in [[Temora, New South Wales]], Australia. He is the son of former [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] player Peter Stimson and nephew of former [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]] and [[Wests Tigers]] player [[Mark Stimson (rugby league)|Mark Stimson]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Les Muir20 Jun 2011, 11:21 p.m. |url=http://www.dailyadvertiser.com.au/story/742782/joe-stimson-and-andrew-heffernan-next-in-line-for-rugby-league-greatness/ |title=Joe Stimson and Andrew Heffernan next in line for rugby league greatness |publisher=The Daily Advertiser |date=2011-06-20 |access-date=2017-03-11}}</ref>

Stimson played his junior rugby league for the [[Temora Dragons]]. He was educated at [[Mosman High School]] before being signed by the [[Melbourne Storm]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

== Playing career ==
=== Early career ===
In 2014 and 2015, Stimson played for the Melbourne Storm's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/s/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160131072555/http://nycdatabase.org/players/s/ |url-status=usurped |archive-date=31 January 2016 |title=S |publisher=Nyc Database |access-date=2017-03-10}}</ref> captaining the side in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|author=Nathan Ryan |url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/news/fresh-blood-from-20s-nsw-and-queensland-cup-to-make-nrl-impact-in-2016/news-story/ced4a509ef565b8784574d2d87f8bb40 |title=Fresh blood to make NRL impact in 2016 |work=Fox Sports |date=2015-10-16 |access-date=2017-03-10}}</ref> In July 2015, he played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/07/04/pay_s_say_nsw_under_.html|title=PAY'S SAY: NSW Under-20s side|publisher=NSWRL.com.au|access-date=2015-07-09}}</ref> In August 2015, he re-signed with the Storm on a 2-year contract until the end of 2017.<ref>{{cite web |author=Melbourne Storm |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/storm-secure-young-talent-18682/ |title=Storm secure young talent |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2015-08-20 |access-date=2017-03-10 |archive-date=12 March 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170312045437/https://www.zerotackle.com/storm-secure-young-talent-18682/ |url-status=dead }}</ref> In 2016, he graduated to the Storm's [[Queensland Cup]] team, [[Sunshine Coast Falcons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cairnspost.com.au/sport/local-sport/rugby-league-falcons-and-pride-coy-over-chances/news-story/c747608b74b159c9136ed605474da657 |title=RUGBY LEAGUE: Falcons and Pride coy over chances |publisher=Cairnspost.com.au |date=2016-03-03 |access-date=2017-03-10}}</ref>

=== 2017 ===
In round 2 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Stimson made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Melbourne side against the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-warriors-v-storm/tabid/10874/newsid/104442/default.aspx |title=Updated team lists: Warriors v Storm |publisher=NRL.com |date=2016-12-18 |access-date=2017-03-10}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL Digital Media |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2017/03/10/stimson_leuluai_to_m.af_news.html |title=Stimson, Leuluai to make Storm debut – Storm |publisher=Melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=2017-03-10}}</ref> A few days later, he re-signed with Melbourne on a two-year contract until the end of 2019.<ref>{{cite web|author=Melbourne Storm |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/bromwich-stimson-re-sign-melbourne-storm-25840/ |title=Bromwich, Stimson re-sign with Melbourne Storm |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2016-09-28 |access-date=2017-03-13}}</ref>

=== 2018 ===
Stimson was part of the Melbourne team that played in the [[2018 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[Sydney Roosters]] but lost 21–6.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Woods |first=Melissa |date=26 September 2018 |title=Storm's Stimson sad to squeeze out hero |url=https://www.espn.co.uk/nrl/story/_/id/24806427/storm-stimson-sad-squeeze-hero |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=ESPN}}</ref>

=== 2019 ===
On 4 June, Stimson signed a three-year deal with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] from 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bulldogs.com.au/news/2019/06/04/joe-stimson-to-join-the-bulldogs-on-a-three-year-deal/|title=Joe Stimson to join the Bulldogs on a three year deal|date=2019-06-03|website=Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|language=en|access-date=2019-06-30}}</ref>

In round 22, Stimson played his 50th NRL game for the Storm in their 18–22 loss to the [[Canberra Raiders]] at [[Melbourne Rectangular Stadium|AAMI Park]] in Melbourne.{{citation needed|date=July 2025}}

=== 2020 ===
Stimson made his debut for Canterbury-Bankstown in round 1 of the [[2020 NRL season]] against arch rivals Parramatta. Canterbury would go on to lose the match 8–2.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/live-nrl-bulldogs-new-halves-combination-put-to-the-test/news-story/bfe1ea0540810e5b7098016b9d150682 |title=Live NRL: After 65 minutes we FINALLY have a try – Eels rookie breaks deadlock |work=Fox Sports |date=12 March 2020}}</ref>

On 29 April, Stimson was ruled out for the entire 2020 NRL season due to a shoulder injury which required surgery.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/nrl-2020-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-kieran-foran-dean-stimson-nrl-injuries-casualty-ward/news-story/f7e3f459229b620de7d7f1727851b04f|title=Bulldogs handed Foran boost as Stimson set to be ruled out for the year|work=Fox Sports |date=30 April 2020}}</ref>

=== 2021 ===
[[File:JOE STIMSON.jpg|thumb|right|Stimson playing for the Mounties in 2021]]
Stimson played 11 games for Canterbury in the [[2021 NRL season]] which saw the club finish last and claim the [[Wooden Spoon (award)|Wooden Spoon]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-st-georgeillawarra-dragons-gold-coast-titans-departing-players-corey-norman-ashley-taylor-tyrone-peachey-transfer-centre-signings/news-story/a6c1cdb196e86219a8075fa120d8ae69/|title=Bulldogs' backline boost as rising star re-signs; Eels hooker seals UK exit: Transfer Centre|website=foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2022 ===
Stimson made a total of 21 appearances for Canterbury throughout the 2022 season. The club would finish 12th on the table and miss the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-canterbury-bulldogs-season-review/wapmyddrbadukioadiybr1fs/|title=NRL 2022: Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

On 30 September, Stimson signed a two-year deal to join the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2022/09/29/joe-goes-north-stimson-a-titan-in-23/|title=Joe ready to go: Stimson a Titan in '23|website=www.titans.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2023 ===
Stimson played a total of 23 matches for the Gold Coast in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-gold-coast-titans-season-review/nel398u2vn7faognqvwnq7g4#:~:text=Did%20the%20Titans%20over%2Ddeliver,out%20of%20the%20finals%20picture./|title=NRL 2023: Gold Coast Titans season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>

=== 2024 ===
Stimson was limited to only six matches for the Gold Coast in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-gold-coast-titans-analysis-exclusive-20240908-p5k8xf.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: Titans 'perennial enigma' who went missing as Des Hasler 'hallmark' falls flat|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Following his departure from the Gold Coast Titans, Stimson joined [[Brisbane Tigers]] in the Queensland Cup, while worked in landscaping and maintenance.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=24 January 2026 |title=Joe Stimson keeps Castleford Tigers challenges in perspective after experiencing life as gardener |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/joe-stimson-keeps-castleford-tigers-challenges-in-perspective-after-experiencing-life-as-gardener-5486818 |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

On 23 June, Stimson signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-and-a-half-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=23 June 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers' new signing confirmed as NRL forward joins with contract length revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-new-signing-confirmed-as-nrl-forward-joins-with-contract-length-revealed |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=23 Jun 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Castleford Tigers announce signing of Gold Coast Titans prop|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-announce-signing-of-gold-coast-titans-prop/}}</ref> He made his Tigers debut on 28 June against [[Wigan Warriors]] in round 16,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=27 June 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers signing Joe Stimson set for debut v Wigan Warriors as Danny McGuire makes 'attack' vow |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-signing-joe-stimson-set-for-debut-v-wigan-warriors-as-danny-mcguire-makes-attack-vow-5198293 |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref> and played a total of 11 games in the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]] as Castleford finished 11th on the table.<ref name="RLRKC" />

=== 2026 ===
Stimson was assigned squad number 13 for 2026.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He scored the first try of [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|Castleford's 2026 season]], his first for the club, against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=26 December 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers reveal 2026 squad numbers with new-look top 17 and shocks confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-reveal-2026-squad-numbers-with-new-look-top-17-and-shocks-confirmed |access-date=8 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>
